title: Simple Book Catalog API
statement:
Create a Node.js backend to manage a catalog of books. The catalog should support adding, retrieving, and listing books. Implement the following API endpoints:

### GET /api/books
- Returns a list of all books in the catalog.

### POST /api/books
- Creates a new book in the catalog.
- Requires a JSON payload with title and author in the request body.
- Returns the newly created book if successful, or an error message if unsuccessful.

### GET /api/books/:bookId
- Returns a book by its unique ID.
- If the book does not exist, returns a 404 status code.

### PUT /api/books/:bookId
- Updates a book in the catalog by its ID.
- Requires a JSON payload with title and author in the request body.
- Returns the updated book if successful, or an error message if unsuccessful.

### DELETE /api/books/:bookId
- Deletes a book by its ID.
- Returns a success message if successful, or an error message if unsuccessful.

You can use an in-memory array to store the books. Ensure that the API returns suitable HTTP status codes and response bodies for each endpoint.
constraints: Must use the in-memory array provided in the starter code to store the books.
Must handle errors and return suitable response bodies for each endpoint.

endpoints.description: Returns a list of all books in the catalog.
Creates a new book in the catalog. Requires a JSON payload with title and author in the request body.
Returns a book by its unique ID.
Updates a book in the catalog by its ID. Requires a JSON payload with title and author in the request body.
Deletes a book by its ID.
